commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a reusable live provider model catalog SDK helper, exports it as a Plugin SDK subpath, and migrates OpenCode Go catalog discovery to use it with refreshed model metadata and docs.

PR surface: Source +342, Tests +256, Docs +11, Config +4, Other +7. Total +620 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The changed source path is reviewable, but after-fix real OpenClaw runtime proof is still insufficient.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are public SDK API acceptance and contributor-owned runtime proof, not a narrow code repair.

target.title: feat: add live provider model catalog helper
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime` so provider plugins can share guarded, cached `/models`-style live catalog discovery.
- Rework OpenCode Go to consume the generic SDK helper instead of carrying provider-local fetch/cache/parser code.
- Refresh OpenCode Go model metadata, including the current OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible endpoint split.
- Wire the new SDK subpath through package exports, boundary checks, and provider-plugin docs.

## How it works
Provider plugins keep provider-owned static model metadata as the source of capabilities, pricing, endpoint family, and fallback rows. In `catalog.run`, a provider can call `buildLiveModelProviderConfig(...)` with its discovery endpoint, provider config, static models, auth marker, and resolved discovery key.

The helper performs an SSRF-guarded fetch, composes caller abort signals with a bounded timeout, parses OpenAI-style `{ data: [...] }` or top-level array responses by default, de-dupes model ids, caches the live result with the shared TTL cache, filters live ids through the provider-owned static metadata, and falls back to the static rows if discovery fails or returns no known models. Providers can override row/header parsing for non-OpenAI-shaped APIs.

OpenCode Go now uses this route for `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models`. If no OpenCode Go/shared OpenCode auth is configured, the live catalog hook returns `null`, so normal/static model browsing stays fast and unchanged. When auth is configured, discovery uses `discoveryApiKey` for the request while preserving the configured `apiKey` marker in the returned provider config.

## Verification
- `curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models | node -e '...'` returned 18 ids, including `qwen3.7-plus`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: provider plugins can now opt into a reusable SDK path for live model discovery; OpenCode Go uses it to refresh available Go models without adding provider-specific hot-path polling.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout against the current OpenCode Go public `/models` endpoint on June 3, 2026.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest files, core/extensions TS checks, scoped oxfmt check, `git diff --check`, full `pnpm build`, live `/models` curl, and branch autoreview against `upstream/main`.

Evidence after fix: the live endpoint returned the expected model ids; SDK/export checks passed during `pnpm build`; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: OpenCode Go live discovery filters known live ids through current provider-owned metadata, preserves static fallback behavior, and the generic SDK helper is available through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime`.

What was not tested: no authenticated completion request was sent for every OpenCode Go model, and no second real provider was migrated beyond the generic helper tests.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
